content: Curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM, RND) and 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agent memory share a hidden inverted synergy: curiosity can be framed as an intrinsic memory pruning signal, where the surprise metrics used to rank exploration in ICM/RND can directly prioritize which novel experiences are stored in SOTA active memory to avoid capacity bloat. This creates a curious agent that actively grows its memory only around high-intrinsic-surprise events, rather than passively storing all encountered data.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
